      Week 1 · Day 1
      Company Overview & Full Product Guide
      0%
      What is Ninja Patches?
      You are becoming THE expert
      Customers will call knowing nothing — about patches, materials, minimums, or how ordering works. You are the person who knows every product, every rule, and every answer. This training assumes you know nothing yet either. That's the point: by the end, you won't be guessing — you'll know this brand inside and out.
      How a Customer Orders on ninjapatches.com
      1
      Customer picks a patch type from the website
      2
      Uploads artwork and customizes: size, shape, border, backing, color, quantity
      3
      Optionally adds a digital proof (free over $300, $19.99 under)
      4
      Checks out — sees the estimated delivery window before paying
      5
      The order lands in our Unreviewed Orders queue — and that's where your job begins

      Ninja Patches specializes in custom heat-pressable patches and branded apparel accessories. Customers can upload artwork, customize patch specifications, approve proofs, and place orders directly through the website. They offer over 15 patch styles along with blank hats and related products.

      Core Selling Points — Know These Cold
      ✓ Free shipping on all orders
      ✓ No setup fees
      ✓ No art fees
      ✓ Low minimum quantities
      ✓ Fast production available
      ✓ Custom shapes & sizes
      ✓ Multiple styles & backing options
      ✓ Bulk discounts available
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      All Patch Types — Know Every One
      Embroidered Patches
      10 MOQ⚡ NinjaFast
      Traditional stitched patches using thread. Classic look with great durability.
      Best for: Business logos, uniforms, hats, clubs & organizations
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Velcro, Peel-and-Stick
      3D Embroidered Patches
      10 MOQ⚡ NinjaFast
      Raised embroidery that creates depth and texture for a premium look.
      Best for: Hat patches, premium branding, bold logo designs
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Velcro, Peel-and-Stick
      Custom Name Patches
      10 MOQ⚡ NinjaFast
      Embroidered name patches, typically woven or embroidered on a base fabric. Classic workwear style.
      Best for: Uniforms, workwear, name tags, staff identification
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Velcro, Peel-and-Stick
      Chenille Patches
      10 MOQ
      Fuzzy, varsity-style patches with a soft raised texture. Iconic letterman look.
      Best for: Letterman jackets, sports teams, school spirit wear
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Velcro, Peel-and-Stick
      Tackle Twill Letters
      25 MOQ
      Cut-and-sewn fabric lettering stitched directly onto garments. Athletic jersey style.
      Best for: Sports jerseys, team uniforms, athletic apparel
      Backings: Sewn-On
      Full Color Printed Patches
      10 MOQ⚡ NinjaFast
      Printed designs with unlimited colors. No thread — crisp digital print on patch material.
      Best for: Photographs, gradients, complex artwork, detailed logos
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Velcro, Peel-and-Stick
      PVC Rubber Patches
      10 MOQ⚡ NinjaFast
      Flexible rubber material, 3D molded design. Highly durable and weather-resistant.
      Best for: Tactical gear, outdoor brands, military-style, bags & gear
      Backings: Velcro, Heat Applied, Peel-and-Stick
      Glow in the Dark PVC
      10 MOQ
      PVC rubber with glow-in-the-dark material. White in light, glows green in the dark.
      Best for: Tactical gear, novelty items, outdoor brands, unique branding
      Backings: Velcro, Heat Applied, Peel-and-Stick
      PVC Rubber Keychains
      10 MOQ
      Same PVC rubber material as patches, formed into keychains with a metal ring. Great branded merchandise.
      Best for: Branded merch, promotional products, events, retail
      Backings: Keychain Ring (no backing)
      Genuine Leather Patches
      10 MOQ
      Real leather with laser-engraved design. Premium feel and natural aging character. Heat-seal only.
      Best for: High-end hats, outdoor brands, luxury branding
      Backings: Heat Applied Only
      Faux Leather Patches
      10 MOQ
      Synthetic leather alternative with laser-engraved design. Vegan-friendly. Heat-seal only.
      Best for: Budget leather look, vegan-friendly branding
      Backings: Heat Applied Only
      Silicone Patches
      10 MOQ⚡ NinjaFast
      Soft-touch modern branding patch. Smooth rubber-like feel with a clean finish.
      Best for: Athletic wear, modern apparel brands, clean minimal branding
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Velcro, Peel-and-Stick
      Metallic Flex Patches
      25 MOQ
      Shiny metallic finish with blue, silver, or gold color options. Eye-catching premium look.
      Best for: Fashion brands, premium apparel, luxury hat branding
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Peel-and-Stick
      Full Color Flex Patches
      25 MOQ
      Full color printing on flex patch material. Combines vivid color detail with the premium Ninja Flex finish.
      Best for: Detailed logos on premium hat branding, colorful designs
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Peel-and-Stick
      Matte Black Flex Patches
      25 MOQ
      Black-on-black matte design. Subtle, sleek, and sophisticated branding.
      Best for: Luxury apparel, premium hat brands, understated branding
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Peel-and-Stick
      Matte White Flex Patches
      25 MOQ
      White-on-white matte design. Clean, minimal, and modern look with subtle embossed detail.
      Best for: Clean minimal branding, white hat labels, modern fashion
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Peel-and-Stick
      Chrome Flex Patches
      25 MOQ
      Mirror-like chrome silver finish. Highly reflective and ultra-premium appearance.
      Best for: High-end fashion, premium branding, standout hat patches
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Peel-and-Stick
      Woven Patches
      10 MOQ⚡ NinjaFast
      Tightly woven threads allow for finer detail than embroidery. Flat, clean finish.
      Best for: Small text, detailed logos, intricate artwork, jean & apparel labels
      Backings: Heat Applied, Iron-On, Velcro, Peel-and-Stick
      Application Instructions — Watch These
      Customers will ask how to apply their patches. Know each method thoroughly before moving on.
      Required: Click each link below to review the instructions. All four must be marked as watched before you can continue to the next section.
      Heat Press / Garments
      Heat Press / Hats
      Iron / Garments
      Peel and Stick
      🔥
      Heat Press for Garments
      How to apply heat-applied patches to t-shirts, jackets, hoodies, and fabric garments using a heat press. Covers temperature, time, and pressure settings.
      Watch
      Reviewed
      🧢
      Heat Press for Hats
      Applying patches to structured and unstructured hats requires different technique than flat garments. Covers hat-specific placement, curved surfaces, and pressure tips.
      Watch
      Reviewed
      🏠
      Iron for Garments
      For customers without a heat press — using a household iron to apply patches. Covers iron settings, parchment paper technique, and tips for best adhesion.
      Watch
      Reviewed
      🩹
      Peel and Stick
      The simplest application method — no heat required. Covers surface prep, how to peel and apply the patch, and tips for hold and longevity.
      Watch
      Reviewed
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      Minimum Order Quantities (MOQ)
      Important: Minimums are per design — 10 or 25 pieces of the same design. A customer cannot split the minimum across multiple different designs.
      10
      Piece Minimum
      • Embroidered Patches
      • 3D Embroidered Patches
      • Full Color Printed Patches
      • PVC Rubber Patches
      • Genuine Leather Patches
      • Faux Leather Patches
      • Woven Patches
      • Chenille Patches
      • Silicone Patches
      25
      Piece Minimum
      • Metallic Flex Patches
      • Matte Black Flex Patches
      • Other Ninja Flex Patch styles
      • Tackle Twill Letters
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      Patch Recommendation Guide
      When a customer asks "what patch should I get?", match their use case below:
      🧢 Hats
      • Leather
      • Faux Leather
      • Embroidered
      • 3D Embroidered
      • PVC
      👔 Work Uniforms
      • Embroidered
      • Woven
      • PVC
      🏃 Athletic Apparel
      • Silicone
      • Printed
      • Tackle Twill
      💎 Luxury Branding
      • Genuine Leather
      • Matte Black Flex
      • Metallic Flex
      🏕️ Outdoor Brands
      • PVC
      • Genuine Leather
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      Backing Options & Heat Press Application
      Available Backings
      🔥 Heat Applied 🏠 Iron-On 🩹 Peel-and-Stick 📎 Velcro
      ⚠️ Note: Leather & Faux Leather = heat-applied only.
      Heat Press Settings
      Temperature340–350°F
      Time20 seconds
      PressureMedium–Firm
      Most patches do not require sewing.
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      Order Workflow — Step by Step
      1
      Select patch type
      Customer chooses from 15+ patch styles
      2
      Enter size and quantity
      Pricing calculator updates automatically
      3
      Upload artwork
      Most file types accepted. Preferred: AI, EPS, SVG, PDF. Acceptable: PNG, JPG
      4
      Choose proof option
      Free over $300 · $19.99 optional under $300
      5
      Complete checkout
      Order enters the system
      6
      Art team reviews design
      Team checks file quality and feasibility
      7
      Digital proof sent via email (if requested)
      A digital mockup is emailed to the customer. Delivered in 1–3 business days. Customers do NOT have to get a proof — if skipped, order goes straight to production.
      8
      Customer approves proof
      Production does NOT begin until approval
      9
      Production begins
      Standard: 9–12 business days · NinjaFast: ~5 business days
      10
      Order ships
      Tracking info sent. Free shipping on all orders.
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      Turnaround Times & Shipping
      📋 About Proofs — Important
      • •Proofs are a digital mockup sent via email for the customer to review before production.
      • •Customers do not have to request a proof. If they skip it, the order moves directly into production.
      • •Free proof on orders over $300. Optional for $19.99 on orders under $300.
      • ⚠Production does NOT begin until proof approval — if a proof was requested. Without a proof, production starts immediately after order placement.
      Standard
      9–12
      business days
      Available on all patch types. Countdown starts after proof approval (or order placement if no proof).
      ⚡ NinjaFast
      ~5
      business days
      Select patch types only. Must be selected at checkout — cannot be added after order is placed.
      Rush Options
      Varies
      by patch type
      Different turnaround tiers available. Use the Patch Calculator to verify exact dates.
      ⚠️ NinjaFast — Critical Rules
      • •NinjaFast can only be selected at checkout when placing the order.
      • •It cannot be added after an order has been placed.
      • •If a customer wants NinjaFast after placing an order: cancel the order first (only if it has NOT moved into production), then re-place with NinjaFast selected.
      Patch Turnaround Calculator
      Use this to give customers accurate ship dates before they place an order
      Open Calculator
      How to use the calculator:
      📌 Tab: "Placed Orders"
      Use this tab when the order has already been placed in the system. Proof options are intentionally removed on this tab. Ignore "Turnaround Rules" — that section is for admins only.
      📋 After selecting the patch type, fill in:
      1Location / ship-to
      2Turnaround time
      3Order date
      4Proof added (yes/no)
      5NDA (Next Day Air)
      6Hit Calculate
      🚚 Shipping Options at Checkout
      Standard Shipping — 9–12 Business Days
      Order will ship between the calculated window. Free on all patch orders.
      FREE
      Expedited Shipping — 7–10 Business Days
      Faster shipping window. Price is calculated at checkout based on order total.
      Calculated at checkout
      Note: These are shipping speed options, not production speed. The ship date shown at checkout accounts for the full production + shipping window. Standard is always free — expedited shipping cost is calculated based on order total.
      Next Day Air (NDA) — Shipping Upgrade Pricing
      Can be added to existing orders if requested. Price is based on order total.
      Order Total (From) NDA Ship Cost
      $0+$35.00
      $99+$45.00
      $299+$55.00
      $499+$65.00
      $999+$85.00
      $1,499+$105.00
      $2,999+$155.00
      $4,999+$205.00
      $9,999+$305.00
      ⚠️ NDA cannot be added to NinjaFast orders. If a customer has a NinjaFast order and wants NDA, it is not allowed — NinjaFast already includes expedited production and the two cannot be combined.
      How to add NDA after order placement: The customer requests NDA upgrade → confirm order has not shipped yet → collect the NDA fee based on the table above → apply the shipping upgrade to the order.
      📣 When they ask "When will my order arrive?"
      "Let me review your order status. Production begins after proof approval — or right after order placement if no proof was requested. Delivery has three stages: proof creation (if applicable), production time, and transit time."
      📣 When they ask "Can I get it sooner?" / "Can I add NinjaFast?"
      "NinjaFast must be selected at checkout when the order is placed — it cannot be added after. If the order hasn't moved into production yet, we can cancel it and re-place it with NinjaFast."
      📣 Standard Timing Script (use this exact wording)
      "Production begins once artwork is approved — or immediately after order placement if no proof was requested. Standard orders ship within 9–12 business days. Qualifying NinjaFast patch types can ship in as little as 5 business days."
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      Objection Handling & Quick Answers
      ❓ "Your price is higher than another company."
      "Our pricing includes professional artwork review, premium materials, no setup fees, and free shipping. We focus on delivering consistent quality and durability."
      ❓ "I only need a few patches."
      "Our minimum quantities start as low as 10 pieces for most patch styles."
      ❓ "Do I have to sew these on?"
      "Most of our patches are heat pressable and designed to apply without sewing."
      ❓ "Can you recreate / clean up my logo?"
      "Yes. The art team reviews every submission and can work with imperfect files. There are no art fees."
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section

      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      Understanding Orders in Shopify
      Every order in Shopify follows the same structure. Learn to read each section so you can assist customers accurately and efficiently.
      Example Order — PATCH-289334
      📋
      Order Number & Header
      Order Number
      Always starts with PATCH- followed by the number — e.g. PATCH-289334
      Date & Time
      Always shown in EST (Eastern Standard Time)
      Action Buttons
      Refund · Edit · Print · More Actions appear at the top right
      Unfulfilled
      The order has not yet shipped to the customer
      📦
      Fulfillment Block
      Shipping Method
      Option chosen at checkout — e.g. Standard Shipping 9–12 Business Days
      Ninja Fast Patches
      Only appears if customer selected NinjaFast at checkout. Eligible patch type alone doesn't trigger it.
      Patch Details
      Patch type name · Unit price · Quantity · Line total
      Artwork Details
      Upload link · Original image · Width/height (inches) · Shape selected
      Backing & Color
      The backing option and color the customer selected
      Design Notes
      Any special instructions the customer added during checkout
      🖼️
      Proof Section
      Free Proof
      Orders over $300 receive a Free Proof — shows as a $0.00 line item labeled "proof-fee"
      Separate Block
      Proof appears as its own unfulfilled block — must be marked fulfilled separately once sent to customer
      Optional Proof
      Orders under $300 can add a proof for $19.99
      💰
      Order Totals
      Subtotal
      Full item price before any discounts
      Discount
      Bulk discount auto-applied — e.g. 50% off for 100+ patches
      Shipping
      Method selected + estimated delivery window shown at checkout
      Taxes
      Tax-exempt customers must upload their form on the website. They receive a confirmation email, then approval/rejection. Cannot refund tax if the form was uploaded after the order was placed.
      📝
      Notes & Customer Info
      Notes
      Internal notes for production/art team. If the order is past "Unreviewed" status, always notify the patch rep/lead when adding a note.
      Customer Name
      Click to open their full account profile
      Order Count
      Click to see all orders under this customer's account
      Email / Addresses
      Shipping can be updated. Billing address cannot be changed once the order has been placed.
      🏷️ Tags — W3 Order Statuses
      The Tags section in the order tells you where the order is in the production process. These are the W3OS statuses you need to know:
      w3os:Unreviewed
      Brand new order — not yet placed with a vendor, no proof in development. Can be canceled or changed freely.
      w3os:Waiting On Proof
      Assigned to a vendor, proof is being developed. Can move to: Proof Approved, Proof Sent to Customer, Waiting on Revision, or Customer Action Needed. Can still be edited, canceled, or changed.
      w3os:Proof Sent to Customer
      Proof received from vendor and sent to customer to review. Ideal time for feedback or cancellation. Can still be edited or changed, but is close to production. Customer can accept (→ Proof Approved) or reject (→ Waiting on Revision).
      w3os:Proof Approved
      Customer or rep has approved the proof. Waiting for vendor to move into production. Can still be edited or changed, but very close to production. Will move to "Sent to Production" next.
      w3os:Waiting on Revision
      Design was rejected and revision requests were sent to the vendor. Changes or cancellations can still be made, but will further delay the order.
      w3os:Sent to Production
      Cannot be canceled or edited. Patches are being made per the approved proof and original quantity. Cannot be expedited. Will move to "Vendor Shipped" when complete.
      w3os:Vendor Shipped
      Production complete, enroute to Ninja for sorting and quality check. Cannot be canceled or expedited. Only allowable change: customer can add UPS Next Day Air shipping — but NOT if they are in Canada, Alaska, or Hawaii.
      w3os:Customer Action Needed
      Temporary hold — order requires customer or rep action such as: confirming a change, sending a new design, updating payment, or other special cases. Can still be edited or canceled.
      w3os:No Proof Send to Prod
      Similar to "Waiting on Proof" — order is pending vendor review before being designed internally and sent to production. Can still be edited or canceled.
      ⚡ Quick Reference — Can I Cancel / Edit?
      Status Cancel? Edit? Add NDA?
      Unreviewed✅✅—
      Waiting On Proof✅✅—
      Proof Sent to Customer✅✅—
      Proof Approved⚠️ Nearly⚠️ Nearly—
      Waiting on Revision✅✅—
      Sent to Production❌❌—
      Vendor Shipped❌❌✅ (excl. Canada/AK/HI)
      Customer Action Needed✅✅—
      No Proof Send to Prod✅✅—
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      SOPs — Unreviewed Orders & Vendors
      These are the SOPs you will use every day when processing orders. Study each one carefully — you will be working with these from Day 3 onwards.
      Unreviewed Orders — Assignment Process
      Order Management

      The Unreviewed Orders dashboard in Periscope is where all new customer orders first appear. The dashboard shows: Shopify Link, Vendor column, Time in Status, Biz Days in Status, and Tags.

      01
      Open Unreviewed Orders in Periscope
      Access the Unreviewed Orders dashboard ↗. Review all orders. Pay attention to Time in Status and Tags.
      Periscope — Unreviewed Orders queue
      02
      Review the Shopify Order
      Click the PATCH-XXXXXX link. Review: patch type, size, shape, border, backing, quantity, artwork, notes, shipping. First-time or repeat? Verify artwork feasibility.
      Example Shopify order view
      03
      Check the Vendor Column
      Sometimes auto-populates. If blank, use the Vendor Chart to find the correct vendor for this patch type.
      Vendor column — blank means assign using the Vendor Chart
      04
      Check Time in Status
      Shows how long the order has been unassigned. Prioritize higher values — delays affect the production timeline.
      Time in Status — higher = longer wait, prioritize these
      05
      Check the Tags Column
      Shows flags: NinjaFast, NEEDS PROOF, Repeat Customer, PATCHSENSEI-MISMATCH, and W3OS status. Review before assigning.
      Tags column — check for NinjaFast, NEEDS PROOF, Repeat Customer
      06
      Assign and Notify Vendor
      Add vendor name as Shopify tag. Notify rep in timeline (or admin site for World). Standard: "@[Rep] We are assigning this order." NinjaFast: "@[Rep] RUSH — ship within 5 days."
      07
      Update Order Status
      Under 25 pcs + no proof → No Proof Sent to Prod · Under 25 pcs + proof → Waiting on Proof · 25+ pcs → always Waiting on Proof
      💡 Orders 25+ pcs always require proof handling.
      08
      Add Your Name to Shopify Tags
      After assigning and notifying the vendor, add your name to the Shopify tags to confirm the order is handled.
      💡 Critical when multiple CSRs are assigning at the same time.
      Add your name after assignment is complete
      Quick Checklist
      ✓Periscope queue reviewed
      ✓Shopify order reviewed
      ✓Vendor column, Time in Status, Tags checked
      ✓Vendor Chart consulted if vendor blank
      ✓Vendor tag added + rep notified
      ✓W3OS status updated
      ✓Your name added last
      Escalate If
      !Artwork not feasible for patch type
      !Repeat customer but vendor unavailable
      !Unusual specs outside workflow
      !Order unassigned 1+ business day
      Vendor Chart & MOQs
      Vendor Management

      The Vendor Chart for Patch Types is the team guide for determining which vendor should handle each patch type. Use it to ensure orders are assigned to the correct vendor, production runs smoothly, and NinjaFast orders go to rush-capable vendors.

      01
      Check the Vendor Chart
      Open the Vendor Chart for Patch Types (linked in your resources). Find the patch type from the Shopify order and identify which vendors can handle it. Green cells indicate preferred vendors. X means vendor cannot handle that type.
      💡 Preferred vendor for Embroidery: Desent. Full Printed: World or Desent. PVC: World (25+ pieces preferred). Silicone: World or Masters.
      02
      Check if First-Time or Repeat Customer
      In Shopify, check the customer order history on the right side. "1 order" = first-time customer (can use any preferred vendor). "2+ orders" = repeat customer — must use the same vendor as the previous order to maintain consistency.
      ⚠️ Using a different vendor for repeat customers can result in color tone, stitching style, or texture differences that the customer will notice.
      03
      Check MOQ Requirements
      Every vendor has minimum order quantities (MOQ). Verify the order quantity meets the vendor MOQ. World: 10 MOQ standard, 25+ for manual placement. Quality Patches: 10 MOQ. Desent: 10 MOQ. Shingian: 25+ pieces for most types. Aspire: 10 MOQ. Masters: 10 MOQ. Penn: 10 MOQ. Versa: 25+ pieces for most types.
      04
      NinjaFast Orders
      For NinjaFast orders, only assign to vendors capable of rush production. Confirm with vendor: "This is a Ninja Fast order that needs to go right to production and ship within 5 business days. Can you meet this timeline?" NinjaFast production is 5 business days after proof approval.
      💡 No proof: countdown starts the day after order is placed. With proof: countdown starts the day after proof approval.
      ⚠️ Shipping upgrades to Next Day Air (NDA) are NOT allowed on NinjaFast orders.
      05
      Assign the Vendor
      Once the correct vendor is identified: (1) Add your name to Shopify tags immediately, (2) Add the vendor name as a tag, (3) Notify the vendor representative in the Shopify timeline or admin website, (4) Update the order status.
      Quick Checklist
      ✓Vendor Chart checked for patch type
      ✓First-time vs repeat customer verified
      ✓MOQ requirements confirmed
      ✓NinjaFast capability confirmed if applicable
      ✓Name added to Shopify tags
      ✓Vendor name added as tag
      ✓Vendor rep notified in timeline
      ✓Order status updated
      Escalate If
      !No vendor can meet the MOQ
      !NinjaFast order but no vendor available for rush
      !Repeat customer vendor no longer available
      !Unusual patch type not on vendor chart
      Vendor Communication SOP
      Vendor Management

      All vendor communication must follow this SOP. Vendor representatives are: Desent → @Jessie Ye (Shopify Timeline & Admin), Aspire → @Saba Azam (Shopify Timeline), Shingian → @Selina Lau (Shopify Timeline & Admin), Penn → @Alison Castillo & @Laura Estela Borjas (Admin), Versa → @Jessica Vanmeter (Admin).

      01
      Use Only Approved Channels
      All vendor communication must go through the designated vendor portal or the approved email thread. Never contact vendor staff via personal messaging, social media, or phone without supervisor approval.
      ⚠️ Using unauthorized channels can void vendor agreements and create liability. Always use official channels.
      02
      Include Required Reference IDs
      Every message to a vendor must include both the Ninja Patches order ID and the vendor's own order ID. This eliminates confusion and speeds up responses.
      💡 Use format: NP-[ORDER_ID] | Vendor: [VENDOR_ID] at the start of every message.
      03
      Standard Status Requests
      Use the "Order Status Follow-Up" template for routine updates. Keep messages concise. One question per message when possible. Always end with a specific request and deadline.
      04
      Monitor Response SLA
      Vendors have a 24-hour response SLA. Set a calendar reminder. If no response in 24 hours, send one follow-up with the same template. Log the follow-up in Shopify.
      05
      Escalate Non-Response at 48 Hours
      If vendor does not respond after 48 hours and two follow-ups, escalate to supervisor immediately. Do not continue attempting contact without supervisor guidance.
      06
      Document All Vendor Interactions
      Log every vendor communication in the Shopify order notes: date, channel used, what was asked, and what the response was. This protects Ninja Patches in all disputes.
      💡 Complete, accurate notes are your best protection if a dispute escalates to management or legal.
      Quick Checklist
      ✓Official channel used (portal or email)
      ✓NP order ID and Vendor order ID included
      ✓Professional tone maintained
      ✓Response SLA tracked (24 hours)
      ✓Follow-up sent if no response in 24 hours
      ✓All interactions logged in Shopify
      Escalate If
      !No vendor response after 48 hours
      !Vendor disputes order details
      !Vendor claims order was not received
      !Repeated quality issues from same vendor
      !Vendor requests information outside normal scope

      Week 1 · Day 3
      Order Processing, Vendors & Proof Review
      Days 1 & 2 Refresher
      Before we move forward, let's make sure the foundations are solid. Review these key points from Days 1 and 2:
      Day 1 Key Points
      • •15+ patch types and their best use cases
      • •MOQs: 10 pcs most types, 25 pcs for Flex & Tackle Twill
      • •No setup fees, no art fees, free shipping
      • •Standard: 9–12 days · NinjaFast: ~5 days
      • •Proofs are optional — skipping goes straight to production
      Day 2 Key Points
      • •All orders start with PATCH- prefix
      • •Add your name to Shopify tags immediately when opening an order
      • •Billing address cannot be changed after order placed
      • •Sent to Production = cannot cancel or edit
      • •NDA not allowed on NinjaFast orders or Canada/AK/HI
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      Practice — Unreviewed Orders & Vendor Assignment
      This section is about getting comfortable with the order assignment workflow. You will do this every single day. The goal is for it to become second nature.
      Periscope — Unreviewed Orders
      Open the unreviewed orders queue
      Vendor Chart for Patch Types
      Which vendor handles which patch type
      About the Vendor Chart
      The Vendor Chart for Patch Types is the team's guide for determining which vendor should handle a specific patch type. Always check this before assigning.
      Patch Types
      Embroidery Full Printed Print + Embroidery PVC · PVC Keychain Silicone Leather + Faux Chenille · 3D Embroidery Name Tag · Tackle Twill Woven · Flex
      Available Vendors
      World Quality Patches Desent Shingian Aspire Masters Penn Versa
      Use the Vendor Chart spreadsheet to confirm which vendor handles each patch type. Green = preferred vendor · X = vendor cannot handle that type. Always confirm NinjaFast capability for rush orders.
      Order Assignment Workflow
      1
      Open Periscope → Unreviewed Orders queue
      2
      Immediately add your name to Shopify tags — prevents duplicate handling
      3
      Review order in Shopify: patch type, quantity, artwork, backing, shipping, first-time or repeat customer?
      4
      Check Vendor Chart → identify correct vendor for the patch type
      5
      Repeat customer? Must use same vendor as previous order for consistency
      6
      Add vendor tag → notify rep in Shopify timeline → update W3OS status
      W3OS Status Rule After Assignment
      Under 25 pcs · No Proof
      "No Proof, Sent to Prod"
      Under 25 pcs · With Proof
      "Waiting on Proof"
      25+ pcs · Any
      Always "Waiting on Proof"
      Review Session — prove you've mastered this section
      Proof Review Cycle SOP
      After an order is assigned to a vendor, it enters the proof cycle. Understanding this process is critical — production does not begin until the proof is approved.
      Proof Review Cycle SOP
      Design & Production

      After assigning to a vendor or placing a new order on the vendor site, orders move into Waiting on Proof status. When the proof is ready, the W3 status updates to Vendor Proof Ready, then Proof Sent to Customer once sent. Standard proof turnaround is 2-3 business days. Proofs cannot be rushed. Production begins only after the proof is approved.

      01
      Receive Proof from Vendor
      When the vendor submits a proof, log it in the order record immediately. Note the timestamp. You have 4 hours to forward it to the customer during business hours.
      02
      Send Proof to Customer
      Use the "Proof Ready for Review" template. Attach the proof file clearly. Set an internal reminder for the 24-hour follow-up if no response is received.
      💡 Always send the proof with a numbered revision count (e.g., "Proof 1 of 2 free revisions").
      03
      Customer Approves Proof
      Log the approval in Shopify with timestamp. Notify the vendor to proceed with production via portal. Move order to "In Production" status.
      04
      Customer Requests Changes
      Document the change request clearly. Submit revision request to vendor with specific change notes. Count this as Revision 1. Vendor must return revised proof within 24 hours.
      💡 Be specific in vendor notes — vague revision requests cause delays and miscommunications.
      05
      Second Rejection — Escalate Check
      If customer rejects proof a second time, notify them that this is their final free revision. Advise the $10 fee for any further revision. Evaluate if the vendor is meeting design specs.
      ⚠️ If vendor is repeatedly failing to match customer's original brief, this is a vendor quality issue — escalate to supervisor.
      06
      Auto-Approval & No Response
      If no response in 72 hours after 2 follow-ups: send final auto-approval notice, log in Shopify, and notify vendor to proceed. Document all follow-up timestamps.
      Quick Checklist
      ✓Proof received and timestamped
      ✓Proof forwarded to customer within 4 hours
      ✓Revision count tracked
      ✓Customer response logged
      ✓Vendor notified of final decision
      ✓Auto-approval process followed if no response
      Escalate If
      !Vendor misses 24-hour proof revision deadline
      !Customer rejects proof 3+ times
      !Proof does not match original customer brief
      !Customer refuses all proofs and demands cancellation
      !Rush orders with proof turnaround under 2 hours

      Proof Statuses — Waiting & Sent
      Waiting on Proof

      Communication Templates
      Approved messaging for every customer and vendor situation. These are official Ninja Patches email and timeline templates.
      Vendor Timeline — Standard Order

      Assigning to Desent (Standard)

      Used when tagging Jessie Ye in Shopify timeline or admin website for a standard order.

      @Jessie Ye We're assigning this order to you. Thank you.
      Vendor Timeline — NinjaFast

      Assigning Rush Order to Vendor

      Used when assigning a NinjaFast/rush order to any vendor representative.

      @[Vendor Rep] This is a RUSH order. Please produce and ship within 5 days. Thank you.
      World Vendor — 24 pcs Below

      World Vendor — No Proof Note

      Used in the Admin website timeline when tagging @Daniel for orders 24 pcs and below with no proof.

      NEW ORDER - No Proof Sent to Prod
      Cancellation & Refund — Embroidery

      Classic Embroidery — Not Viable

      When embroidery is not viable due to high detail at scale or gradients in the design.

      Hey there, Thank you for your interest in Ninja Patches Embroidery. After reviewing your order, we have determined that embroidery is not viable for the design you submitted due to high detail at scale or the presence of gradients in your design. We have canceled this item and issued a credit to your account. You are welcome to place an order using a more accommodating product type like Full-Color Printed, or submit a revised embroidery design.
      Cancellation & Refund — Chenille

      Chenille — Not Viable

      When chenille is not viable due to level of detail and scale.

      Hey there, Thank you for your interest in Ninja Patches Chenille. After reviewing your order, we have determined that chenille is not viable for the design you submitted due to the level of detail and scale. We have canceled this item and issued a credit to your account. You are welcome to place an order using a more accommodating product type like Full-Color Printed, or submit a revised chenille design (ideally at a larger scale).
      Cancellation & Refund — Woven

      Woven — Not Viable

      When woven patches are not viable due to high detail or gradients.

      Hey there, Thank you for your interest in Ninja Patches Woven. After reviewing your order, we have determined that woven patches are not viable for the design you submitted due to high detail at scale or the presence of gradients in your design. We have canceled this item and issued a credit to your account. You are welcome to place an order using a more accommodating product type like Full-Color Printed, or submit a revised woven design.
      Cancellation & Refund — Leather

      Leather/Faux Leather — Not Viable

      When leather engraving is not viable due to high detail, scale, or multiple colors.

      Hey there, Thank you for your interest in Ninja Patches Leather. After reviewing your order, we have determined that leather engraving is not viable for the design you submitted due to high detail, scale, or multiple colors in your design. We have canceled this item and issued a credit to your account. You are welcome to place an order using a more accommodating product type like Full-Color Printed, or submit an alternative leather-compatible design.
      Proof — Single Stitch Warning

      Single Stitch Quality Notice

      When a proof comes back with single-stitch text — send to customer before production.

      Due to the scale requested for this patch the text could only be embroidered in a "single-stitch" style. This can often lead to an illegible, poor quality look in the final product.
      Escalation — Customer Insists on Hold

      Cannot Keep Customer On Hold

      When a customer insists on waiting for a supervisor while on the call.

      I'm unable to keep you on the line while waiting for a Supervisor/Manager to become available, but I have escalated your request. I will take down your information and follow up with you as soon as possible.
      Escalation — Customer Refuses Info

      Customer Won't Provide Account Info

      When a customer refuses to provide identifying information but requests a manager.

      I understand you'd like to speak with a manager. I can definitely look into that for you, but I'll need a bit of information so I can pull up your order and get you to the right person.
      NDA Shipping

      Reason for Edit — NDA Upgrade

      Used in Shopify when editing shipping to upgrade to Next Day Air.

      Cx upgrade to NDA
      Cancellation & Refund — Puff Transfer

      3D Puff Transfer — Not Viable

      When a Puff Transfer order cannot be produced because the design is multicolor.

      Hey there, Thank you for your interest in Ninja Transfers 3D Puff Transfers. We have canceled and refunded your Puff Transfer order. Your design is a multicolor image which cannot be produced as a Puff Transfer. 3D Puff transfers can only be produced as single-color prints and work best with minimal detail. We highly suggest reviewing examples on the Puff Transfer page to determine whether your artwork is a good fit before placing a new order.
      Policies & Rules
      Official Ninja Patches operational policies. These are the rules agents must follow.
      Shopify Order Status Reference (W3os)
      Unreviewed
      New order — not yet placed with a vendor. Can be canceled or changed.
      Waiting on Proof
      Assigned to vendor, waiting on proof development. Can be edited, canceled, or changed.
      Proof Approved
      Proof approved for production. Very close to production — still editable but act fast.
      Proof Sent to Customer
      Customer reviewing proof. Ideal time for feedback or cancellation. Still editable.
      Waiting on Revision
      Design revision requested. Changes can be made but will delay further.
      Sent to Production
      CANNOT be canceled or edited. Patches are being made per last approved proof.
      Vendor Shipped
      Finished, in transit to Ninja for quality check. Cannot cancel. Only NDA upgrade allowed (US only).
      No Proof, Sent to Prod
      24 pcs below, no proof — order can proceed directly to production. Still editable/cancelable.
      Customer Action Needed
      Order on hold — customer needs to confirm change, send new design, or update payment.
      Proof Timing & Policies
      Turnaround
      Standard proof turnaround: 2–3 business days
      Free Proofs
      Proofs are free on orders over $300. If customer with order over $300 opts out, remove and notify service channel.
      No Rush
      Proofs cannot be rushed under any circumstances.
      Production Start
      Production begins ONLY after proof is approved.
      Vendor Always Proofs
      The vendor will always send a proof whether or not the customer purchased one.
      25 pcs+
      Orders 25 pcs and above always go to "Waiting on Proof" status regardless of proof purchase.
      NinjaFast Policy
      Definition
      Rush production program for accelerated turnaround. Rush fee calculated at checkout.
      Timeline
      5 business days after proof approval. Typically 6-10 business days total delivery after proof approval.
      No proof countdown
      Starts the day AFTER order is placed.
      With proof countdown
      Starts the day AFTER proof approval.
      NDA Not Allowed
      Shipping upgrades to Next Day Air are NOT allowed on NinjaFast orders.
      Vendor Confirmation
      Must confirm vendor can meet timeline: "This is a Ninja Fast order that needs to go right to production and ship within 5 business days. Can you meet this timeline?"
      Upgrade Approval
      Do NOT offer NinjaFast upgrades without approval from Mercedes, Michel, or Gabriel.
      NDA (Next Day Air) Shipping Policy
      Eligibility
      All 4 must be true: (1) No tracking on order yet, (2) Not NinjaFast or Expedited, (3) Within Contiguous US, (4) Standard turnaround
      Exclusions
      Canada, Alaska, Hawaii — NDA NOT available
      NinjaFast/Expedited
      Cannot adjust shipping — already has the quickest shipping available
      Reason for Edit
      Always enter "Cx upgrade to NDA" as the reason for edit in Shopify
      Escalation Policy
      When to Escalate
      ALL 3 must be true: (1) Customer requests Supervisor/Manager, (2) You have provided all available info, (3) You have consulted Team Leads
      Where
      Post in the #ninja-transfers-service Slack channel
      Include
      Customer account information + clear summary of the issue + reason for escalation
      Do NOT
      Do not keep the customer waiting on the line for a Supervisor/Manager
      Lost in Transit Policy
      Definition
      No tracking update for 3-4 business days = potentially lost in transit
      Resolutions
      Reprint order (same turnaround), Credit the order, or Refund the order
      $500+ Orders
      Must obtain team lead approval BEFORE offering a refund as resolution
      Tax Exempt Policy
      Submission
      Customers upload tax-exempt forms directly on the website
      Review
      Form sent to Accounting/Tax department for review — they email the customer with approval or rejection
      Until Approval
      Customers continue to be charged tax on orders until they receive official approval email
      Vendor Assignment Rules
      First-Time Customer
      Can be assigned to any preferred vendor based on the Vendor Chart for Patch Types
      Repeat Customer
      MUST be assigned to the same vendor who handled the previous order — different vendors have slight differences in color tones, stitching style, border stitching, material texture, and print output
      Reorder Process
      Check customer's previous order → identify original vendor → assign new order to same vendor
      Add Name to Tags
      Immediately add your name in Shopify tags when opening an order to prevent duplicate handling

      Full platform management — content, credentials, and configuration.
